open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own tab). Load boundaries still lack a numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and a processing timeout. Consumers of this class: (1) viewer.js loadDocument() renders one canvas per page (linear; render-race amplifier FIXED+VERIFIED in TASK-316); (2) thumbnails.js (TASK-322/323, reviewed this tick) renders a SECOND canvas per page into the Pages panel — confirmed browser-side at 6 canvases for a 6-page doc, doubling the per-page footprint of an uncapped high-page-count PDF; (3) merge.js (TASK-319) caps each file at 50MB but not cumulative queued bytes/pages; (4) watermark.js (TASK-321) re-loads the open doc into pdf-lib (operates only on the already-50MB-validated doc, not a new external boundary). thumbnails.js mitigates its own footprint (CSS thumb width 120px, dpr capped at 2, sequential render, renderToken guard) but the page COUNT is still unbounded. Single right fix = developer task: a page-count/byte cap at the viewer.js LOAD boundary protects viewer + thumbnails + every future per-page consumer at once. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.
